headlines: Bitcoin ETFs post first monthly inflows since October
Franklin Templeton to buy CoinFund spinoff, launch 'Franklin Crypto'
SoFi launches 'Big Business Banking' on Solana for fiat + stablecoin rails

CFTC wins consent order: KuCoin operator barred from US access, $500K penalty (consent order) — March 30, 2026 filing noted in coverage)  *date clarification included* )​**(Regulatory/Exchange)**:  — (CFTC press release)  — (Benzinga)​)​**(Regulatory/Exchange)**  — (CFTC press release)  — (Benzinga)**

articles.title: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs posted $1.32B in March — first monthly inflow since October
articles.summary: March saw U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs attract about $1.32 billion, ending a four-month outflow streak. The inflows show some returning institutional interest but Q1 still finished with net outflows overall. (Data and coverage cited Apr 1, 2026.)

articles.title: Bitcoin fell 24% in Q1 — worst first quarter since 2018
articles.summary: Bitcoin closed Q1 2026 down roughly 24%, its weakest opening quarter in years as macro pressure and ETF flow swings weighed on prices. The drop highlights ongoing market stress even as ETFs and institutional buyers show mixed signals. (Reported Apr 1–2, 2026.)

articles.title: Franklin Templeton to buy CoinFund spinoff 250 Digital and form 'Franklin Crypto'
articles.summary: Asset manager Franklin Templeton agreed to acquire 250 Digital (a CoinFund spinoff) and will roll the team into a new institutional unit called Franklin Crypto, boosting traditional-finance exposure to active crypto strategies. The deal was announced Apr 1, 2026.

articles.title: SoFi launches 'Big Business Banking' that uses Solana for enterprise fiat and stablecoin operations
articles.summary: SoFi unveiled 'Big Business Banking' — a regulated, 24/7 enterprise banking platform that will leverage Solana (and other rails) to let companies hold deposits, move fiat, mint/burn stablecoins and settle in real time. The launch was announced Apr 2, 2026.

articles.title: CFTC consent order bars KuCoin operator from U.S.; $500,000 civil penalty
articles.summary: The CFTC’s consent order against Peken Global Limited (operator of KuCoin) requires a $500,000 penalty and prohibits allowing U.S. participants to trade on KuCoin unless the platform registers with the CFTC as a foreign board of trade. (Court action and CFTC release published Mar 30–31, 2026; widely reported Apr 1.)

articles.title: Ripple’s RLUSD stablecoin lists on South Korea exchange Coinone
articles.summary: Ripple’s dollar-pegged stablecoin RLUSD was listed on Coinone, giving Korean traders KRW access to RLUSD trading and expanding Ripple’s stablecoin reach in Asia. (Reported Apr 1, 2026.)

articles.title: Large corporate treasuries keep buying Ethereum — big weekly ETH buys reported
articles.summary: On-chain trackers show corporate and treasury buyers continued large ETH purchases in late March–early April (weekly seven-figure buys), signaling institutional accumulation even as ETH prices remain under pressure. (Reported Apr 1, 2026.)

articles.title: Ethereum ETF complex and some ETH funds saw outflows as April opened; Grayscale products bucked the trend
articles.summary: Spot Ethereum ETFs finished Q1 with notable outflows and some ETF products saw further withdrawals on Apr 1, 2026 — while Grayscale’s ETH product attracted inflows, highlighting fee- and product-level rotation among institutional investors.
